sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, is experiencing neck pain after a recent car crash in February of this year.
He reports the pain is sharp, incapacitating, and rated as a 12 out of 10.
He has a history of multiple car crashes, including one where he underwent surgery, and has been experiencing pain since the most recent crash.
He has been to therapy and a pain clinic, where he was prescribed fentanyl, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
He reports experiencing fatigue, headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms throughout his body.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one where he underwent surgery, and has been experiencing neck pain since the most recent crash.
Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue, headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: Fentanyl (dose and frequency not specified) for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the bony process and muscle in the neck, limited range of motion, and is unable to bend forward or backward without pain.
There is no bruising or swelling noted.
Test Results: X-ray results show no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is a diagnosis made based on the patient's symptoms and physical exam findings, and is not a fracture as initially suspected.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation and pain management.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy and order an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
I will also refer the patient to pain medicine for further evaluation of local injections.
The patient will be given a note to provide to their employer to excuse them from work until further notice.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again after the MRI results are available, at which time further management and treatment will be discussed.
